title: Specifying podman socket with podman cli.
body:
I noticed that now we have podman api socket.
Does/will it support something like this?
```
podman system service -t 0 tcp://0.0.0.0:5192
```
```
podman -H tcp://0.0.0.0:5192 ps
```

comments.body:
I'll defer to @rhatdan here, but I believe the answer is no - we're reluctant to add support for that as it will require us to provide authentication for the socket.

Instead, we're focusing on tunneling requests to the unix socket over SSH.

I've already tried podman-remote through varlink with ssh, and it works perfectly.
Then I found this that varlink is going to be deprecated, so i assumed no ssh client in podman which is not necessarily bad news to me. Because that means I have an option to use tcp directly or set up ssh tcp forwarding with openssh.

 https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/source/markdown/podman-system-service.1.md
We're definitely integrating SSH tunnelling into the remote Podman client, and I believe the Go bindings for the HTTP API as well (@jwhonce can confirm whether I'm right about that).
Also varklink is deprecated.  Not going away for a while.
I think this is adequately answered, so I'm going to close - if there are further questions, please open a fresh issue.
